nuggets.headline: pete rango's musical path began with self-taught guitar and a metal/hardcore band
nuggets.text: Before he was crafting tracks like "Oms at Play," Pete Rango's musical foundation was laid in unexpected ways. Born in Bogota, Colombia, he taught himself guitar at age nine after clashing with his teacher, and later formed a metal/hardcore band called In Alcatraz 1962 in high school. This early experience in a band became the catalyst for him to learn how to record, mix, and produce his own records, setting the stage for his future in music production.

source.title: Meet Pete Rango - Voyage MIA Magazine | Miami City Guide
source.quoteSnippet: I was born in Bogota, Colombia. I became interested in music when I was nine years old. I started teaching myself how to play guitar because I couldn’t get along with my teacher. ... When I was in high school, I started a metal/hardcore band called In Alcatraz 1962, and that became the catalyst for me wanting to learn how to record/mix/produce our own records.

nuggets.imageCaption: Pete Rango, known for his production work, playing bass.

nuggets.headline: pete rango's productions have appeared in shows on hbo, bet, and vh1
nuggets.text: Beyond his work on albums, Pete Rango has a significant presence in television and film, with numerous sync placements for his music. His tracks have been featured in a variety of popular shows, including HBO's "DTF St. Louis" and "Rap Sh!t" Season 2, BET's "Tyler Perry's Sistas," and VH1's "My Celebrity Dream Wedding." This extensive behind-the-scenes work highlights his versatility as a producer whose sound resonates across different media platforms.

source.title: Pete Rango
source.quoteSnippet: Music featured in TV shows, films, commercials, and other media: DTF St. Louis - HBOView, Rap Sh!t - HBO Max Season 2View, Tyler Perry's Sistas - BETView, My Celebrity Dream Wedding - VH1View

nuggets.imageCaption: whose sound resonates across different media platforms

nuggets.headline: if you're digging this, you need to check out nia sultana
nuggets.text: If Pete Rango's unique production style is grabbing you right now, then you absolutely need to add Nia Sultana to your playlist. Both artists have worked with Saki Engineering for their mixing, mastering, and production needs, meaning there's a shared sonic expertise behind their tracks. You can hear that high-quality, meticulous approach in both their catalogs, making Nia Sultana a perfect next listen.

nuggets.imageCaption: Nia Sultana, an artist with a shared production connection to Pete Rango.

artistSummary: Pete Rango is a music producer, creative director, and Web3 consultant originally from Bogota, Colombia. His diverse career spans from early punk rock roots to extensive sync placements in television, and he's also known for his work as a live audio engineer.
